content:
Retrieve the latest (2024‑2025) official documentation for authenticating to GitHub with a Personal Access Token. Include:
1. API section – how to add the `Authorization: token <PAT>` header.
2. UI section – using the token to automatically fill the login form on https://github.com/login and what GitHub expects (use of `input[name='login']`, `input[name='password']` → the token is not directly accepted in the form; we must inject it as a value for a hidden field if the form supports it, otherwise discuss why a real UI login needs a user to enter username/password first and then exchange the token for a session cookie.
3. Anti‑bot measures – mention reCAPTCHA, “login.gov” redirects, and the fact GitHub may block tokens used from headless browsers. Provide at least one production‑grade example (e.g., a Node script that uses Puppeteer to sign in with a PAT after the user typed their username/password once, then copies the resulting cookies). Include URLs (no tutorials, focus on official repos from GitHub’s own code base or their developer docs). 
Skip generic blog posts. 
Return the collected markdown in a single block, one bullet per section, with links.
<!-- OMO_INTERNAL_INITIATOR -->
